title: thymus serpyllum subvar. ericoides
intro: Thymus serpyllum subvar. ericoides, commonly known as creeping thyme, is a low-growing, spreading variety of the thyme plant, characterized by its small, fragrant leaves and pink or purple flowers. The medicinal properties of creeping thyme include its antimicrobial and antiseptic properties, which make it effective in treating wounds, infections, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of creeping thyme include its ability to reduce stress and anxiety, improve cognitive function, and aid in digestion, as well as its potential to boost the immune system and fight off diseases. Creeping thyme can be consumed as a tea, taken as a tincture, or added to cooking and herbal remedies, making it a versatile and accessible herb for various health and wellness purposes.
